findingDetails.title: Fresh daemon bootstrap can ignore an explicit embedding provider override
Cancelling a running embedder reindex does not stop the worker before completion

findingDetails.title: Lazy model-server respawn locks expire while the live listener still owns them
findingDetails.claim: The demand listener holds the respawn lock for the whole listener lifetime, but stale detection reclaims locks older than 60 seconds even when the owner PID is alive.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked release sites and cross-launcher tests; immediate contention is covered, but no test covers a live-owner lock older than RESPAWN_LOCK_STALE_MS.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The age threshold is valid for orphan cleanup of short critical sections, but it conflicts with the documented long-lived lazy listener lock.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if another guard prevents a second launcher from reaching acquireDemandRespawnLock after 60 seconds while the first listener remains alive.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A lock intentionally held for the lazy listener lifetime can be age-reclaimed while the owner process is alive.

searchActions.result: RESPAWN_LOCK_STALE_MS is 60000; isRespawnLockStale reclaims age-old locks even after confirming only that dead PIDs are stale; comments say the lock is intentionally held across the listener window.
Immediate second launcher blocking is covered; live-owner aged-lock blocking is not.

searchLedger.rationale: The stale policy contradicts the long-lived lock ownership contract and can reopen duplicate listener races.

searchLedger.invariant: Shutdown should drain watcher work before checkpointing and closing the vector DB/WAL.

searchActions.result: fileWatcher.close is awaited before vectorIndex.closeDb.

searchLedger.rationale: No new shutdown/WAL ordering finding in this pass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Drain-before-close and checkpoint-before-close ordering are present in the reviewed code.

searchLedger.rationale: No new standalone-save finding beyond DR-002-P1-002.

graphEvents.label: P1 demand spawn failure strands lazy listener

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Final stabilization pass spans model-server demand listener ownership, respawn lock lifetime, launcher cross-process election, workflow locks, and embedding transport/security surfaces.

findingDetails.title: Demand-triggered model-server spawn failures strand the lazy listener
findingDetails.claim: handleModelServerDemand removes the demand listener before it knows the real model server launched. If launch returns false because the child has no PID, later clients have no lazy listener even though the response reports loading; if launch throws synchronously, releaseDemandRespawnLock is skipped.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked model-server unit and cross-launcher tests for failed spawn/no-PID demand coverage and for a finally-protected lock release path; found success-path and contention coverage but no failed-launch listener re-arm assertion.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Spawn failure may be rare with process.execPath and many child_process failures are emitted asynchronously, but launch explicitly returns false for a no-PID child and the demand handler currently treats that as a loading response after removing the listener.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if supported runtimes guarantee spawnFn cannot return a no-PID child or throw synchronously, or if another production path immediately re-arms the demand listener after launched:false.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A demand-triggered launch failure can remove the lazy listener without re-arming it and can skip lock release on synchronous launch errors.

searchActions.result: Demand path clears/unlinks the listener before launch, keeps the respawn lock, then releases the lock only after launch returns.
launch can return false when the spawned child has no valid pid.
Tests cover successful lazy launch, cross-launcher lock contention, and booting-window backoff, but no no-pid/failed demand launch recovery.

searchLedger.rationale: A failed launch should fail closed or re-arm the listener; the current ordering can leave neither a listener nor a model-server child.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The live-owner respawn lock may still be age-reclaimed after 60 seconds.

searchActions.result: Confirmed the same live-owner age-reclaim behavior from iteration 5.

searchLedger.rationale: The issue remains active but is already captured by a prior finding.

searchLedger.invariant: A sibling launcher should not bind or spawn while another live listener or live booting model server owns the socket.

searchActions.result: Prepare path checks live socket health, holds the demand respawn lock, respects a live recorded pid during spawn-to-bind, and releases the lock on listen failure.
Immediate second launcher and booting-window cases are covered.

searchLedger.rationale: No new cross-launcher election race beyond DR-005-P1-001 and DR-006-P1-001 was found in this pass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct read and existing tests cover the immediate contention and booting-pid election branches.

findingDetails.title: Non-active tracked SQLite connections skip the TRUNCATE checkpoint on shutdown

searchLedger.invariant: Cache eviction should enforce global, per-profile, and query byte budgets while preserving shard-qualified table access.

searchActions.result: cacheTable switches to active_vec when attached; migration, lookup recency, store, and LRU byte-budget deletion all use cacheTable(db).
Tests cover fresh schema, legacy backfill, scoped lookup, global/profile/query byte caps, shrink_memory, and legacy lookup compatibility.

searchLedger.rationale: No new cache eviction or shard-table migration defect found in this pass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Reviewed implementation and targeted tests cover the named byte-budget/LRU and shard-qualified table behavior.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Shutdown may apply the TRUNCATE checkpoint sequence only to the active singleton, leaving prior tracked custom DB connections to detach/close without the explicit durability guarantee.

searchActions.result: initialize_db(custom_path) creates tracked connections; close_db loops all tracked conns but only checkpoints the active db after non-active conns are closed.
Existing tests assert active DB and active shard checkpointing, and separately exercise switching active DBs, but do not assert checkpointing for every tracked connection on shutdown.

searchLedger.rationale: The same close path that explicitly rejects passive close as insufficient for the active DB relies on passive close for every non-active tracked DB.

searchLedger.invariant: Fatal shutdown should drain file-watcher work before closing the vector DB so drains cannot reopen DB after the TRUNCATE checkpoint.

file-watcher close path tracks in-flight tasks and rejects queued work during close, matching the shutdown drain design.

searchLedger.rationale: The daemon-level ordering invariant is present and covered; the new WAL finding is confined to vector-index-store's handling of multiple tracked DB connections.

searchLedger.rationale: No new retention sweep correctness defect found.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The requested post-delete FTS optimize and incremental_vacuum guards are implemented and tested.

Tests cover byte-budget splitting, batched POST row counts, null-slot preservation, truncated response rejection, and one-time dimension adoption for custom models.

graphEvents.label: P1 reindex shard writer skips WAL TRUNCATE checkpoint

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Final sweep spans the whole embedding-stack hardening scope and revisits WAL lifecycle, provider selection, dimension adoption, batching, cache, and vector persistence paths.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected last-chance hotspots named in the iteration prompt plus direct consumers that prove whether provider fallback or dimension drift can persist wrong-sized vectors.

findingDetails.title: Reindex shard writer bypasses the WAL TRUNCATE durability path
findingDetails.claim: writeVectorsToShard opens a direct WAL-backed shard connection, writes vector rows, and closes without the explicit TRUNCATE checkpoint required elsewhere for at-rest durability.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked for connection tracking, checkpointAllWal, per-shard wal_checkpoint before close, and post-reindex attach checkpointing; none are present.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: SQLite WAL is recoverable with the WAL file, but the hardening program explicitly treats passive close as insufficient for at-rest durability.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the durability contract is narrowed to daemon shutdown only or passive close is accepted for reindex shard writers.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A reindex-created shard connection can write WAL-backed vector data and close without the explicit TRUNCATE checkpoint required by the vector-index close path.

searchActions.result: writeVectorsToShard opens a new Database, enables WAL, writes vectors, and closes the shard without wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE).
The active pointer flips after the shard write and attachActiveVectorShard attaches the shard, but no checkpoint occurs in that transition.
Checkpoint tests cover vector-index-store active close and retention, not the reindex shard writer.

searchLedger.rationale: This is distinct from DR-008-P1-001 because the shard connection is never registered in db_connections and therefore cannot be fixed only by checkpointing all tracked connections.

searchLedger.invariant: HF local must adopt server/custom dimensions once, reject row-count or row-dimension mismatches, normalize vectors, and preserve batch ordering/null slots.

searchActions.result: adoptEmbeddingDimension reads server dimension, validates first row and every mapped row, l2-normalizes returned vectors, and embedBatch maps prepared outputs back to original indexes.

searchLedger.rationale: No new dimension-adoption or normalization finding.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Implementation validates response shape and dimensions before returning vectors.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Partial provider availability could silently switch to a different-dimension provider and persist incompatible vectors.

searchActions.result: Bootstrap auto-select remains local-first: ollama, hf-local, openai, voyage.
Factory fallback records dimensionChanged metadata and returns the fallback provider; it warns but does not itself persist vectors.

searchLedger.rationale: Provider fallback sharp edge exists, but persistence/search paths separately validate embedding length before vector writes.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No evidence that fallback dimension drift bypasses vector write validation in this scope.

searchLedger.invariant: Wrong-sized embeddings must not be marked success or inserted into active vec tables.

searchActions.result: index and update paths reject embeddings whose length differs from get_embedding_dim before vector insertion/success marking.
Cache lookup is dimension-keyed and generated cache writes store actual embedding length; execution router resolves dimensions from override/profile.

searchLedger.rationale: No new data drop/duplication path from wrong-dimensional embeddings in the reviewed persistence path.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Dimension checks fire before vector insert/success state changes.

searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Dispose waits on tracked in-flight runs before native session disposal.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Shutdown or periodic checkpointing could run after detach/close and reopen or double-close the vector index.

searchActions.result: fatalShutdown is single-entry guarded, drains fileWatcher first, then runs vectorIndex.closeDb(), and clears timers later.
close_db checkpoints tracked connections before detach/close, clears connection map, nulls active db, and checkpointAllWal returns when db is null.
The previously open direct shard writer WAL issue is fixed by a finally-block checkpoint before shard close.
Tests assert watcher drain precedes close and close/checkpointAllWal issue the expected WAL checkpoint calls.

searchLedger.rationale: No checkpoint-after-detach or double-close finding found at line level; any interval after close sees null db and returns.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Shutdown order and close/checkpoint guards are present.

searchLedger.hypothesis: One of the three open P2 advisories may actually be a P1 correctness/security or release-gate issue.

searchActions.result: Direct binary still has weaker EADDRINUSE reclaim, but supervised production path refuses live-resident unlink.
The spec matrix has a stale file pointer, while tasks and implementation summary document the corrected supervision-layer implementation location.
ENV_REFERENCE still omits several code-visible embedding env knobs, while code reads them directly.

searchLedger.rationale: The P2s remain advisory: operational footgun, noisy traceability, and incomplete env docs; none shows a direct P1 correctness/security failure in this pass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No P2 has new evidence of release-blocking behavior.

focus: daemon shutdown + WAL ordering line-level

graphEvents.label: P1 clean-shutdown marker deleted before close completes

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Line-level review of shutdown/WAL ordering across context-server shutdown, timer checkpoints, vector-index close, shard detach, and unclean-shutdown marker lifecycle.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected the shutdown producer, vector-index WAL/marker owner, timer registry used by periodic checkpointing, and existing tests that claim the shutdown invariants.

findingDetails.title: Clean-shutdown marker is deleted before shard detach and DB close can fail

searchLedger.hypothesis: The unclean-shutdown marker may be removed before all close stages complete, causing the next boot to skip integrity detection after an incomplete shutdown.

searchActions.result: Marker is written on DB open; close removes it after main checkpoint succeeds but before shard detach and db.close.
detachActiveVectorShard performs DETACH outside close_db's local catch and can throw after marker removal.
Boot FTS integrity check is skipped entirely when the marker is absent.
Tests cover checkpoint ordering and marker-gated boot checks, but not marker removal after successful detach/close.

searchLedger.rationale: The marker contract protects the next boot's corruption check; deleting it before detach/close completion can hide an incomplete shutdown.

searchLedger.invariant: Shutdown should not run close_db twice or checkpoint a closed handle after successful close.

searchActions.result: fatalShutdown has a single-entry shuttingDown guard and closes vectorIndex before clearing timers.
close_db clears tracked connections, nulls active db after close, and checkpointAllWal returns if db is null.

searchLedger.rationale: No double-close or successful-close use-after-close path found in this pass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Single-entry shutdown guard plus checkpointAllWal null guard cover the reviewed path.

searchLedger.invariant: Shard WAL checkpoint must occur before detaching the active vector shard.

searchActions.result: Non-active and active close paths attempt shard checkpoint before detachActiveVectorShard.
Test asserts active shard checkpoint precedes DETACH.

searchLedger.rationale: The reviewed close path checkpoints before detach.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Line ordering and tests match the invariant.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The periodic checkpoint interval may fire after vectorIndex.closeDb but before timers are cleared.

searchActions.result: Timer clearing occurs after vectorIndex close, so a post-close interval tick is possible during later awaited cleanup.
checkpointAllWal checks !db before issuing WAL pragmas, so successful close converts interval ticks into no-ops.
clearAllTimers cancels all registered interval/timeout handles once reached.

searchLedger.rationale: A post-close interval tick can occur, but it is guarded by the null db check after successful close.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No use-after-close behavior for the successful close path.

focus: cache + reindex + router deep: eviction/store ordering, partial chunk failure, profile-key drift

graphEvents.label: P1 router adapter cache omits embedding dimension

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Iteration 013 rechecks cache migration/eviction, reindex batch/shard writes, and router dimension/profile isolation across committed embedding-stack code.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Prompt-selected high-risk surfaces: cache eviction/migration/profile key behavior, reindex batch loop and shard write ordering, and execution-router count+byte chunking/profile dimension isolation.

findingDetails.title: Factory-backed adapter cache ignores dimensions, so same provider/model profile swaps can reuse the wrong embedding dimension
findingDetails.claim: getEmbedderAdapter resolves dimensionsOverride but caches factory-backed adapters under provider:model only. If the same provider/model is used with a different active dimension in the same process, the router returns the old adapter/provider, while callers and cache profile keys believe they are operating under the new dimension.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked router tests for provider/model rotation, dimension resolution, batching, and ready exposure. They assert cache keys such as openai:model-a and voyage:model-b, but do not cover two calls with the same provider/model and different dimensions. Cache lookup/storage does include profile_key and dimensions, which protects stored rows but does not force the router to construct a dimension-correct provider.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Production might never switch the same provider/model between dimensions without clearing process state. The public router accepts dimensionsOverride, active profiles include dimension, and query callers derive profile keys from active dimension, so that assumption is not enforced at the boundary.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if all production callers prove same provider/model cannot be requested with different dimensions in one process, or if profile activation always calls clearEmbedderExecutionRouterState before any same-model dimension change.

searchLedger.invariant: Cache writes should enforce byte budgets after store without corrupting scoped lookup state or evicting unrelated fresh profile data first.

searchActions.result: storeEmbedding writes one scoped row, then runs global/profile/query/entry eviction in LRU order.
Tests cover global, per-profile, query budget enforcement and shrink_memory on deletion.

searchLedger.rationale: No new eviction-vs-store bug found; the post-store enforcement can delete old rows and is covered for the intended budget classes.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct read and targeted tests show scoped rows remain distinct and LRU budget trimming executes after every store.

searchLedger.invariant: Legacy and shard cache migration should preserve or backfill profile_key/input_kind so scoped lookups do not collide across profiles.

searchActions.result: Fresh migration recreates legacy cache with profile_key/input_kind and backfills profile metadata when present.
Shard migration preserves existing profile_key/input_kind when columns exist and defaults missing legacy columns to document profile empty.
Tests cover main legacy backfill and active-shard scoped cache writes/lookups.

searchLedger.rationale: No new cache migration finding; existing migration/default behavior matches legacy-compat expectations and scoped shard writes are covered.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The risky profile/input-kind columns are either preserved/backfilled or intentionally defaulted for legacy rows, with direct tests for main backfill and shard scoped writes.

searchLedger.invariant: A failed batch should not mark the job completed or flip the active embedder unless all rows are embedded and written.

searchActions.result: The loop checks batch cardinality before writes, increments processed only after main and shard writes, and runs active-embedder/status completion in a later transaction.

searchLedger.rationale: No new partial-chunk completion bug found; failures before completion leave the job failed rather than completed or active-swapped.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Completion metadata and active embedder flip happen only after the batch loop exits normally.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The direct adapter cache may conflate same provider/model profiles that differ only by dimension.

searchActions.result: getEmbedderAdapter resolves dimensions but caches and rotates adapters under provider:model only.
Factory-backed adapter/provider captures the resolved dimensions at construction.
Query embedding derives profileKey from active dimension, obtains an adapter with embeddingDim, then stores returned embeddings under that profileKey.
Tests cover dimension resolution and provider/model rotation but no same provider/model with changed dimensions.

searchLedger.rationale: Dimension is part of the active embedding profile and cache identity, but the router cache omits it, so same-model dimension changes can reuse stale providers.

searchLedger.invariant: Router batching should split by count and bytes and throw before returning partial/null embeddings.

searchActions.result: Router builds non-empty chunks under max count and byte budget, calls embedBatch once per chunk, and throws on null slots.
Tests cover count splitting, byte splitting, order preservation, and null rejection.

searchLedger.rationale: No partial chunking bug found in the router batch assembly/return path.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct read and tests cover count cap, byte cap, output order, and null failure.

focus: retention + governance transaction-boundary sweep

graphEvents.label: P1 retention sweep stale-candidate delete can override rescued delete_after

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Iteration 014 targets destructive retention deletion, governance audit/history writes, FTS maintenance, vacuum, WAL checkpointing, and cross-process/file-backed writer transaction boundaries.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected the retention sweep implementation and its direct delete, audit, ledger, handler, scheduler, and test consumers because the prompt focuses on destructive delete transaction boundaries and post-delete maintenance ordering.

findingDetails.title: Retention sweep deletes stale candidates without revalidating delete_after inside the transaction
findingDetails.claim: Expired rows are selected before the sweep transaction, then deleted by id inside the transaction without confirming the row is still expired, so a concurrent retention-policy update can be lost as a destructive delete.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether candidate selection occurs inside sweepTx, whether deleteMemory enforces a delete_after predicate, whether handler/session callers serialize retention sweeps behind a visible single-writer guard, and whether tests cover updating an already-selected expired row before deletion.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Same-process JavaScript cannot interleave between selection and transaction without an await, but file-backed/multi-process writers remain possible and the existing test suite already models another file-backed writer for retention behavior.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if all memory metadata updates and retention sweeps are proven to run under a process-wide exclusive writer lock, or if selection and deletion are moved into the same immediate write transaction with an expiry predicate.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A row selected as expired can have delete_after extended or cleared before the sweep transaction deletes it, but the sweep still deletes the stale id.

searchActions.result: Expired rows are selected before sweepTx, and sweepTx deletes each candidate id without rereading delete_after.
delete_memory_from_database deletes memory_index by id; no delete_after or retention predicate is enforced in the lower delete path.
Manual and scheduled retention sweeps invoke the same function on the live database without a visible transaction-local candidate refresh.
Concurrent coverage inserts a future row through another connection, but does not update an already-selected expired candidate before deletion.

searchLedger.rationale: The destructive retention predicate is not transaction-local, so governance updates can be overwritten by a stale retention delete.

searchLedger.invariant: FTS optimize, incremental vacuum, and WAL checkpoint should run only after the delete transaction commits.

searchActions.result: runPostDeleteMaintenance is called after sweepTx returns and only when deletedIds is non-empty.
Tests assert optimize, auto_vacuum, and wal_checkpoint run with db.inTransaction false and are skipped when no rows are deleted.

searchLedger.rationale: No maintenance-ordering bug found; the calls run after transaction completion.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct code and tests prove post-delete maintenance occurs outside the sweep transaction.

searchLedger.invariant: Primary memory, vector, cache, FTS-triggered, and ancillary deletes should not partially commit before the sweep transaction can roll back.

searchActions.result: The sweep wraps deletes in sweepTx; delete_memory_from_database performs vector/ancillary/cache deletes before deleting memory_index by id, and FTS cleanup is trigger-driven from the primary delete.
Only BM25 document removal happens after the delete transaction returns, and it is explicitly best-effort after a successful primary delete.

searchLedger.rationale: No separate partial-primary-row corruption finding beyond the stale-candidate predicate bug.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Primary SQLite deletes remain inside transaction scope; post-transaction BM25 cleanup is non-authoritative and best-effort.

searchLedger.invariant: Retention deletes should leave at least one durable governance/audit signal and report ledger failure to callers.

searchActions.result: History and governance audit writes occur in the sweep transaction; mutation-ledger append is attempted after deleted ids accumulate.
Handler reports a hint when mutation ledger append fails instead of hiding ledgerRecorded=false.

searchLedger.rationale: No new governance-ledger P1 found; ledger failure is surfaced and governance audit/history rows remain transaction-bound.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The review did not find an unreported delete path from ledger failure.

focus: embedder-status handler + shared/types contract + bridge probe health field

graphEvents.label: P1 embedder_status drops model load progress timestamps

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Iteration 017 targets non-trivial status payload and shared contract boundaries across the MCP handler, provider metadata type, live hf-model-server health payload, launcher bridge probe, and idle-monitor consumers.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected the producer/consumer chain for embedder_status modelServer payload correctness: handler, shared metadata contract, provider health parser, live health producer, bridge probe, idle health consumer, and the tests that encode the currently divergent assumptions.

findingDetails.title: embedder_status drops live model-server load timestamps because the shared metadata contract uses the wrong type
findingDetails.claim: The local model server publishes loadStartedAt/loadProgressAt as millisecond numbers and the bridge probe consumes them that way, but HfLocalProvider only accepts string timestamps and ProviderMetadata types them as string|null. During a live loading/ready health check, applyHealthMetadata ignores the numeric fields, so embedder_status serializes null progress fields even though the server health payload contains the data.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether the server emits ISO strings instead of numbers, whether the bridge treats strings as canonical, whether embedder_status bypasses HfLocalProvider metadata, and whether tests exercise the live server payload rather than a mocked provider. The server tests assert numbers, the bridge uses Number.isFinite on the fields, and embedder_status only returns HfLocalProvider.getMetadata after healthCheck.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The strings in embedder-status.vitest could represent the intended public MCP shape, but that is not what the committed live model server emits, and no conversion layer exists between /api/health and ProviderMetadata.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if loadStartedAt/loadProgressAt are explicitly non-contractual for embedder_status consumers, or if another layer not reviewed here converts the live numeric health payload into strings before HfLocalProvider.applyHealthMetadata runs.

searchLedger.hypothesis: embedder_status modelServer progress fields can be present in /api/health but absent from the returned MCP payload because producer and metadata contract disagree on timestamp type.

searchActions.result: The model server stamps loadStartedAt/loadProgressAt with Date.now() numbers and returns those fields directly from healthPayload.
The bridge probe treats loadProgressAt/loadStartedAt as finite millisecond numbers when deciding whether loading is wedged.
ProviderMetadata declares loadStartedAt/loadProgressAt as string|null, and applyHealthMetadata only stores them when the health payload fields are strings or null.
The embedder_status test uses mocked ISO strings, while the live model-server tests assert numeric timestamps, so no test covers the real integration contract.

searchLedger.rationale: The same health fields are numeric in both producer and bridge consumer, but the MCP status path silently filters them out by type before serializing modelServer metadata.

searchLedger.invariant: embedder_status should still return reindex job status when provider resolution is invalid or non-local.

searchActions.result: Provider info collection is isolated in a try/catch and returns modelServerError without aborting job status mapping.
Unsupported EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER throws at provider validation, which the handler catches in collectEmbeddingsStatus.
Test covers graceful degradation when getProviderInfo throws.

searchLedger.rationale: No new handler-crash finding found for provider misconfiguration or non-local providers.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The handler catches provider resolution failures and keeps job status in the response.

searchLedger.invariant: Adding health fields such as health, inFlight, and lastSuccessfulEmbedAt should not make older probe payloads unsafe.

searchActions.result: probeModelServer passes parsed.body as optional health only for alive ready/loading responses and otherwise keeps the legacy status/reason shape.
Idle monitor treats health as optional and returns when lastSuccessfulEmbedAt is absent or invalid, preserving compatibility with older probe results.
Current server emits lastSuccessfulEmbedAt, inFlight, and queueDepth additively in the health payload.

searchLedger.rationale: The additive bridge health object is backward compatible; absent health data disables idle reap rather than causing an unsafe reap.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Consumers null-check health and required fields before acting.

searchLedger.invariant: Consumers that can run before provider initialization should not require full ProviderMetadata fields.

searchActions.result: The shared facade returns ProviderInfo before providerInstance exists and ProviderMetadata only after initialization.
The memory health surface uses PartialProviderMetadata with optional healthy/model/dim fields for partial provider metadata.
ProviderMetadata remains the full initialized provider contract; the concrete bug found in this pass is timestamp type drift, not optional healthy/model handling.

searchLedger.rationale: No additional P1 found for general ProviderMetadata optionality; partial consumers use a partial type and the facade advertises ProviderMetadata | ProviderInfo.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The reviewed pre-initialization consumers do not require full ProviderMetadata.

focus: scripts daemon-detect lease-path resolution + isProcessAlive; workflow Step-11.5 daemon guard + lock semantics

graphEvents.label: P1 Step 11.5 daemon guard ignores live childPid in stale launcher lease
can re-open standalone indexer while daemon child is alive

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Line-level re-review of daemon lease detection, Step 11.5 daemon guard behavior, and workflow lock fail-open semantics in non-trivial save/indexing code.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Prompt-selected scripts plus producer/consumer evidence for the mk-spec-memory launcher lease payload and the tests that currently define daemon-detection coverage.

findingDetails.title: Step 11.5 daemon guard ignores live childPid in a stale launcher lease
findingDetails.claim: isSpecMemoryDaemonAlive only checks the launcher lease pid/ownerPid. The launcher lease also records childPid for the context-server daemon, but Step 11.5 ignores it, so a hard-killed launcher can leave a live context-server child holding the daemon database while standalone save concludes daemonStatus.alive=false and starts reindexSpecDocs as a second writer.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked the launcher normal shutdown paths and tests. Signal handlers kill child processes and clear the lease, uncaughtException clears the lease, and current daemon-detect tests cover live pid, dead pid, missing lease, and canonical lease path. I did not find a test where lease.pid is dead but lease.childPid is live, nor logic that checks childPid before allowing Step 11.5 standalone indexing.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: A clean launcher shutdown should clear the lease and stop the child, and same-process runs are serialized by the workflow queue. The gap is the hard-death/stale-lease case: SIGKILL or process death before cleanup can leave the spawned context-server reparented while the lease still contains the live childPid that the detector ignores.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the context-server child is proven unable to survive launcher hard death on supported runtimes, or if Step 11.5 is otherwise protected by an exclusive writer lease that covers the live child process before reindexSpecDocs runs.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A stale launcher lease can still name a live daemon child, but Step 11.5 may only probe the dead launcher pid and then run a standalone indexer.

searchActions.result: Lease reader returns parsed.pid or ownerPid only; isSpecMemoryDaemonAlive probes only that pid.
Launcher lease payload includes childPid when context-server is spawned, but the detector ignores that field.
Step 11.5 runs reindexSpecDocs whenever daemonStatus.alive is false.
Tests cover live/dead launcher pid and daemon-up skip, but no dead launcher pid with live childPid lease.

searchLedger.rationale: The guard preserves single-writer safety only if the launcher pid is the complete daemon liveness signal; the lease schema says it is not.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The workflow lock may still fail open after timeout or unexpected lock errors.

searchActions.result: Global and per-folder locks still return false and callers still execute operation after failed acquisition.
Same workflow-lock fail-open issue was already reported as DR-002-P1-002 / SEC-2 and re-adjudicated in iteration 007.

searchLedger.notApplicableReason: Existing open finding DR-002-P1-002 / SEC-2; not re-reported as new.
searchLedger.rationale: The fail-open code path remains present, but this iteration treats it as prior art rather than a new finding.

searchLedger.invariant: Default daemon lease path should resolve to system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database in both source and dist layouts.

searchActions.result: Path resolver walks up to the system-spec-kit root and joins mcp_server/database, with a fallback for dist/core versus source layout.
Regression test asserts the default path ends with system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.mk-spec-memory-launcher.json and not scripts/mcp_server.

searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Canonical lease path resolution is implemented and covered for the prior layout regression.
searchLedger.rationale: No new lease-path bug found in this pass.

searchLedger.invariant: When daemon detection misses contention, Step 11.5 should at least surface known database contention errors as warnings instead of swallowing them.

searchActions.result: SQLITE_BUSY, vector_index null, and embedding_cache UNIQUE errors are converted to daemon/index contention warnings.
Test asserts SQLITE_BUSY import failure produces daemon/index contention warning and MCP follow-up.

searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The fallback diagnostic path exists and has direct coverage for SQLITE_BUSY.
searchLedger.rationale: No new contention-warning bug found; this fallback mitigates diagnosis, not second-writer prevention.

focus: ADVERSARIAL re-verification of seven open P1 findings

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Adversarial re-verification spans seven open P1s across provider bootstrap, reindex cancellation, TCP transport/auth, workflow locks, daemon packet evidence, and model-server lease lifecycle.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Prompt explicitly requested refutation attempts for EMB-1, EMB-2, SEC-1, SEC-2, TRC-1, LEASE-1, and LEASE-2; selected the cited producer files and nearby counterevidence surfaces.

findingDetails.title: Fresh daemon bootstrap can ignore an explicit embedding provider override
Cancelling a running embedder reindex does not stop the worker before completion
TCP model-server targets can expose unauthenticated embed traffic outside loopback
Workflow locks fail open after timeout, re-admitting concurrent standalone writers
Daemon child packets leave required verification gates open while claiming implementation status
Lazy model-server respawn locks expire while the live listener still owns them
Demand-triggered model-server spawn failures strand the lazy listener

searchLedger.hypothesis: A fresh daemon bootstrap can persist a local-first auto-selected active embedder before explicit provider resolution runs.

searchActions.result: ensureActiveEmbedder still runs before resolveStartupEmbeddingConfig.
ensureActiveEmbedder delegates to local-first autoSelectActiveEmbedder, while resolveProvider honors explicit EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER.
No earlier explicit-provider injection into ensureActiveEmbedder found.

searchLedger.rationale: Counterevidence search did not find an explicit-provider guard before the persisted active-embedder bootstrap.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A running reindex marked cancelled can keep writing vectors and then complete.

searchActions.result: runJob loops on the initial total and writes/completes without rereading cancelled status; cancelJob marks non-terminal jobs cancelled.
No in-loop cancellation token, status reread, or abort path found in runJob.

searchLedger.rationale: The public cancel path still accepts running jobs but the worker still lacks a stop condition.

searchLedger.hypothesis: TCP model-server targets remain unauthenticated and not limited to loopback.

searchActions.result: Server normalizes tcp/http targets, binds the configured host, and exposes POST /api/embed without auth.
Client accepts tcp/http targets and posts embedding inputs without a bearer/header credential.
No remote-TCP opt-in, loopback allowlist, or request auth guard found for the model-server embed channel.

searchLedger.rationale: The only default-host fallback is 127.0.0.1 when hostname is empty; explicit non-loopback hosts are still accepted without authentication.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Filesystem lock timeout or lock acquisition errors allow write operations to proceed unlocked.

searchActions.result: Workflow lock and per-folder lock return false on timeout/non-EEXIST errors; both wrapper functions run operation regardless.
No caller-side hard abort on false lock acquisition found; unlocked operation remains the fallback.

searchLedger.rationale: The lock wrappers still fail open across process boundaries.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Daemon child packet task/checklist ledgers remain unreconciled with implementation-summary verification claims.

searchActions.result: 013 verification and completion tasks remain unchecked while implementation-summary claims build, focused vitest, live-daemon check, review, and strict validation passed.
Other daemon children still leave strict validation or completion rows unchecked.

searchLedger.rationale: The completion evidence remains internally contradictory; no authoritative reconciliation was found.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A live demand listener's respawn lock can still be reclaimed after the age threshold.

searchActions.result: isRespawnLockStale treats age over 60000ms as stale even when liveness is not dead; the demand listener intentionally holds the lock across its listener lifetime.
No alternate long-lived listener lock exemption or live-owner age bypass found.

searchLedger.rationale: The stale predicate still conflicts with the documented long-lived demand-listener lock lifecycle.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Demand-triggered launch failure can remove the lazy listener and skip lock release on synchronous launch errors.

searchActions.result: Demand listener is released before launch; launch can return false for no PID; releaseDemandRespawnLock sits after launch with no finally.
No failed-launch re-arm or finally-protected lock release around the demand launch path found.

searchLedger.rationale: The failure and synchronous-throw paths remain able to strand the listener/lock lifecycle.

focus: security sweep across secret leakage, path traversal, env precedence/injection, sandbox boundaries, tcp/loopback exposure, and unauthenticated surfaces

graphEvents.label: treats model-server TCP exposure as existing SEC-1

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Security sweep covers multiple process, transport, env, logging, socket, model-server, and workflow-lock surfaces across the embedding-stack hardening program.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected security-critical configured-scope files for transport binding, unauthenticated request surfaces, env forwarding, provider validation/logging, socket directory perimeter, and cross-process writer locks.

searchLedger.invariant: Validation and status paths should not print API key, token, password, or private-key values into logs or MCP responses.

searchActions.result: Hits were provider names, env variable names, status/error messages, and stack logging; no direct API key value interpolation found in reviewed paths.
Validation cache key contains the key only in process memory; log output uses validation.error/provider and does not print process.env.* key contents directly.

searchLedger.rationale: No new direct credential leakage sink was found in the reviewed logging/error paths.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct read found no interpolation of API key/token/password values; remaining logs expose provider names, error messages, paths, and status details only.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The model server may still expose unauthenticated embed traffic on configured TCP targets.

Client accepts tcp/http targets and posts model/input without a credential header.

searchLedger.rationale: The issue remains active but is not new for iteration 019.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Deduplicated against existing DR-002-P1-001 rather than re-reporting SEC-1.

searchLedger.hypothesis: SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR=tcp:// may expose the full MCP daemon over unauthenticated TCP in production.

searchActions.result: Production context-server obtains its socket path from resolveIpcSocketPath(DATABASE_DIR) before startIpcSocketServer; that route does not preserve a tcp:// env value into the daemon listener.
Tests exercise direct TCP bridge/listener behavior, but that is not the production context-server env path.

searchLedger.rationale: The helper supports TCP, but the reviewed production context-server path does not expose the daemon through SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR=tcp://.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No production in-scope call path was found that passes a tcp:// daemon IPC listener target into startIpcSocketServer.

searchLedger.invariant: Socket directory creation and reclaim should fail closed on symlinked or foreign-owned directories and should not turn env paths into unsafe unlink targets.

searchActions.result: Supervised demand listener canonicalizes, asserts socket-dir ownership, rejects symlink/foreign dirs, and reasserts before stale-socket reclaim.
Direct binary still mkdir/unlinks without the launcher guard, but this is the existing direct-startup-socket-unlink P2 path.
Direct startup weaker reclaim is already tracked as DR-001-P2-001.

searchLedger.rationale: No new supervised sandbox/path traversal finding was found; the direct binary weakness remains an existing P2 advisory.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The high-risk supervised listener path has ownership/symlink checks; direct startup was deduped to the prior P2.

searchLedger.invariant: Launcher child env forwarding should not propagate cloud credentials or arbitrary attacker-controlled env into child processes.

Workflow fail-open locking remains an existing security/correctness issue, but no separate env injection path was found in this sweep.

searchLedger.rationale: The launcher does not forward arbitrary env or cloud credentials to the child process in the reviewed path.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Env propagation is allowlisted; the risky forwarded fields are configuration selectors already covered by the transport findings.

focus: final cross-cutting completeness: state-machine gaps, retry idempotency, error-path data loss, and honestly-gated deferrals

graphEvents.label: P1 same-dimension reindex mutates active shard before commit

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Final pass spans committed embedding-stack state machines across reindex, active vector shard attachment, search source selection, WAL close paths, model-server supervision, workflow locks, and spec/documentation gates.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected cross-phase producers where active embedder state, vector writes, failure handling, retry/idempotency, and documented deferrals intersect; excluded already-fixed WAL defects from new finding reporting while verifying their fixes exist.

findingDetails.title: Failed same-dimension reindex can partially overwrite the active vector shard before the model switch commits
findingDetails.claim: runJob writes target vectors through the currently attached vec_<dim> alias before the completion transaction flips the active embedder pointer. For same-dimension/no-op reindex, a later failure or cancellation can leave a mixed active search table under the old active metadata.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether reindex writes only to a staging shard before commit, whether active queries ignore the temp alias until completion, whether failures roll back prior batch writes, and whether same-dimension/no-op targets are rejected before queuing.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The current registry is narrow, so many switches are different-dimension or no-op; still, the public model-switch/reindex contract is generic and the same-dimension path mutates live search rows before the job is known to have succeeded.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the public reindex entry point is guaranteed unreachable for active-name/active-dimension targets or if unqualified vec_<dim> writes are proven not to resolve to the active shard alias under an attached profile.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A same-dimension reindex can write target vectors into the active shard before the active pointer flip, then fail and leave partial live vectors.

searchActions.result: runJob writes each batch to tableName before the completion transaction and failure only marks the job failed.
the attached active profile creates temp vec_<dim> aliases whose INSERT trigger writes to active_vec.vec_<dim>.
vector search reads active_vec.vec_<activeDim> for non-default active embedders and joins rows with embedding_status='success'.

searchLedger.rationale: The switch is not atomic for active-dimension targets because the live vector table is mutated before success is known.

searchLedger.invariant: The previously fixed WAL findings must remain fixed and not be re-reported.

searchActions.result: reindex shard writer now explicitly runs wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) before close.
close_db checkpoints tracked non-active connections and active main/shard connections before close.

searchLedger.ruledOutReason: WAL-1 and WAL-2 fixes are present in committed code.
searchLedger.rationale: No WAL finding emitted in iteration 020.

searchLedger.invariant: Flag flip, dtype, and cache-into-reindex are documented gates/deferrals, not defects.

searchActions.result: parent spec gates flag-flip/live perf/dtype; 004 defers cache-into-reindex; 005 keeps flag default-off, leaves q8 unchanged, and re-defers cache pending normalization fix.

searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The gated items are explicitly documented with blocking conditions and follow-up ownership.
searchLedger.rationale: No finding emitted for flag-flip, dtype, or cache-into-reindex.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Final pass might uncover a distinct state-machine defect adjacent to the already-open P1 clusters.

searchActions.result: Observed prior open provider-bootstrap, running-cancel, workflow-lock, and lease/listener lifecycle clusters; no duplicate emitted.

searchLedger.notApplicableReason: Those clusters are already captured by prior findings and were not re-reported as new.
searchLedger.rationale: DR-020-P1-001 is distinct because it concerns pre-commit active-vector mutation on failed same-dimension reindex.

focus: deep-granularity embed-path and daemon shutdown/WAL re-examination

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Iteration 010 re-examines high-risk saturated surfaces at line level across server/client embedding error ordering, native in-flight disposal, daemon shutdown, periodic WAL checkpointing, and open P2 escalation candidates.

targetSelection.selectionReason: The iteration prompt selected embed-path error/ordering and daemon shutdown/WAL surfaces as highest-risk uncovered-depth targets, plus all three open P2 escalation candidates.

searchLedger.invariant: Malformed batch output must fail the whole embed request before partial rows can be returned or persisted.

searchActions.result: Server validates batch dims/length before constructing response rows and returns 500 on any batch exception.
Client rejects row-count mismatch before mapping and only fills prepared indexes after all embeddings validate.

searchLedger.rationale: No partial-batch data-loss path found; malformed batches fail before result mapping.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Both server and client validate response shape before returning rows.

searchLedger.invariant: Dimension drift must fail before a mixed-dimension batch is returned.

searchActions.result: Single and batch server paths set initial dimension once and throw on later mismatch.
Client adopts server dim from response and validates every row against the active dim before returning normalized Float32Array values.

searchLedger.rationale: No mixed-dimension persistence path found in the reviewed embed response path.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Dimension mismatch throws before any caller receives the batch.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A transient first-batch failure could permanently disable batch inference or skip readiness re-probe.

searchActions.result: Retryable transport codes are not treated as array-input unsupported; fallback latch only sets false for unambiguous capability errors.
Mid-request retryable failures clear serverState and readyLatch before a bounded retry.

searchLedger.rationale: No new latch bug found; the latch only downgrades on capability evidence and readiness retry clears stale state.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Transport failures propagate or retry without mutating supportsArrayInput to false.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Disposal could close native sessions while inference is still using them.

searchActions.result: Raw native runs are tracked in a Set; close destroys active sockets; dispose waits for all tracked runs up to a bounded timeout before native session disposal.

searchLedger.rationale: No use-after-close path found for tracked native inference; timeout is fail-closed as a rejected dispose.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Dispose waits on tracked in-flight runs before native session disposal.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Shutdown or periodic checkpointing could run after detach/close and reopen or double-close the vector index.

searchActions.result: fatalShutdown is single-entry guarded, drains fileWatcher first, then runs vectorIndex.closeDb(), and clears timers later.
close_db checkpoints tracked connections before detach/close, clears connection map, nulls active db, and checkpointAllWal returns when db is null.
The previously open direct shard writer WAL issue is fixed by a finally-block checkpoint before shard close.

searchLedger.rationale: No checkpoint-after-detach or double-close finding found at line level; any interval after close sees null db and returns.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Shutdown order and close/checkpoint guards are present.

searchLedger.hypothesis: One of the three open P2 advisories may actually be a P1 correctness/security or release-gate issue.

searchActions.result: Direct binary still has weaker EADDRINUSE reclaim, but supervised production path refuses live-resident unlink.
The spec matrix has a stale file pointer, while tasks and implementation summary document the corrected supervision-layer implementation location.
ENV_REFERENCE still omits several code-visible embedding env knobs, while code reads them directly.

searchLedger.rationale: The P2s remain advisory: operational footgun, noisy traceability, and incomplete env docs; none shows a direct P1 correctness/security failure in this pass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No P2 has new evidence of release-blocking behavior.

focus: daemon shutdown + WAL ordering line-level

graphEvents.label: P1 clean-shutdown marker deleted before close completes

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Line-level review of shutdown/WAL ordering across context-server shutdown, timer checkpoints, vector-index close, shard detach, and unclean-shutdown marker lifecycle.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected the shutdown producer, vector-index WAL/marker owner, timer registry used by periodic checkpointing, and existing tests that claim the shutdown invariants.

findingDetails.title: Clean-shutdown marker is deleted before shard detach and DB close can fail

searchLedger.hypothesis: The unclean-shutdown marker may be removed before all close stages complete, causing the next boot to skip integrity detection after an incomplete shutdown.

searchActions.result: Marker is written on DB open; close removes it after main checkpoint succeeds but before shard detach and db.close.
detachActiveVectorShard performs DETACH outside close_db's local catch and can throw after marker removal.
Boot FTS integrity check is skipped entirely when the marker is absent.
Tests cover checkpoint ordering and marker-gated boot checks, but not marker removal after successful detach/close.

searchLedger.rationale: The marker contract protects the next boot's corruption check; deleting it before detach/close completion can hide an incomplete shutdown.

searchLedger.invariant: Shutdown should not run close_db twice or checkpoint a closed handle after successful close.

searchActions.result: fatalShutdown has a single-entry shuttingDown guard and closes vectorIndex before clearing timers.
close_db clears tracked connections, nulls active db after close, and checkpointAllWal returns if db is null.

searchLedger.rationale: No double-close or successful-close use-after-close path found in this pass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Single-entry shutdown guard plus checkpointAllWal null guard cover the reviewed path.

searchLedger.invariant: Shard WAL checkpoint must occur before detaching the active vector shard.

searchActions.result: Non-active and active close paths attempt shard checkpoint before detachActiveVectorShard.
Test asserts active shard checkpoint precedes DETACH.

searchLedger.rationale: The reviewed close path checkpoints before detach.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Line ordering and tests match the invariant.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The periodic checkpoint interval may fire after vectorIndex.closeDb but before timers are cleared.

searchActions.result: Timer clearing occurs after vectorIndex close, so a post-close interval tick is possible during later awaited cleanup.
checkpointAllWal checks !db before issuing WAL pragmas, so successful close converts interval ticks into no-ops.
clearAllTimers cancels all registered interval/timeout handles once reached.

searchLedger.rationale: A post-close interval tick can occur, but it is guarded by the null db check after successful close.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No use-after-close behavior for the successful close path.

title: Clean-shutdown marker is deleted before shard detach and DB close can fail

claim: The clean-shutdown marker can be removed before the database shutdown sequence actually completes.
counterevidenceSought: A local catch/finally around detachActiveVectorShard or db.close that preserves/recreates the marker on failure; a test asserting marker removal only after close succeeds; a boot check that runs even when the marker was prematurely removed.
alternativeExplanation: The code may intentionally define marker cleanup as main WAL checkpoint success only, not full database close success.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the marker contract is explicitly only main WAL checkpoint success, or if DETACH/db.close failure is proven impossible in this attached-shard lifecycle.

focus: model-server-supervision + bridge lease/lifecycle deep

graphEvents.label: P1 idle eviction clears ownership without root termination

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: The pass re-examined cross-process model-server supervision, idle eviction, respawn locks, give-up cooldown, and bridge liveness probes under the configured deep-review lifecycle dimension.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected the supervision state machine and bridge probe files named by iteration 12, then added focused tests that cover idle eviction, cross-launcher respawn, and respawn-lock stale behavior as counterevidence.

findingDetails.title: Idle eviction clears ownership without terminating the model-server root process
findingDetails.claim: The idle eviction path treats the model server as evicted after calling reapProcessTree(pid), clearing the shared pid, nulling the supervisor, and attempting lazy re-arm, but reapProcessTree only signals descendants and explicitly excludes the root childPid.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether reapProcessTree signals the root, whether clearTimers kills the child, whether idle tests assert root termination, whether the explicit respawn path behaves differently, and whether bridge probe logic prevents clearing ownership while the root remains alive.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The helper name suggests process-tree reaping and descendant process groups may handle child workers, but the implementation filters out the root pid and idle eviction never sends a separate root signal before clearing ownership.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if another production mechanism proves the root model-server process exits before writeModelServerPid(null) in every idle eviction path.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Idle eviction can clear model-server ownership without terminating the root model-server process.

searchActions.result: reapProcessTreeGroups derives candidate pids from descendants and filters out childPid, so a root-only process tree produces no root termination signal.
tickIdleMonitor calls supervisor.reapProcessTree(pid), clears the shared pid, nulls state.supervisor, then re-arms the lazy listener.
Idle test's processRowsRunner returns only the root pid and asserts pid clearing/re-arm, while the explicit respawn test proves root SIGTERM/SIGKILL on the separate reapBeforeRespawn path.

searchLedger.rationale: The idle path clears ownership and re-arms without proving the root process exited; this can leave an unsupervised model-server resident and weaken the single-writer lease lifecycle.

searchLedger.hypothesis: LEASE-1 may require escalation above P1 after re-reading the current code.

searchActions.result: Confirmed prior live-owner age-reclaim behavior: a lock older than RESPAWN_LOCK_STALE_MS is stale even when the holder pid is live, while comments describe a long-lived listener lock.
Current tests explicitly preserve aged-live lock reclaim behavior.

searchLedger.rationale: Severity escalation ruled out: the finding remains a required availability/single-writer fix but not an observed P0 data-loss/auth-bypass condition.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No evidence that the stale lock race directly causes destructive data loss or exploitable authorization bypass; retain prior P1 classification instead of re-reporting.

searchLedger.hypothesis: LEASE-2 may require escalation or a sibling new finding after re-reading demand spawn failure handling.

searchActions.result: Confirmed prior demand path still releases listener before launch, launch can return false for no pid, and lock release is not finally-protected around synchronous launch failure.

searchLedger.rationale: Severity escalation ruled out: LEASE-2 remains P1 because it can strand lazy spawning, but the reviewed evidence still points to availability/lifecycle breakage rather than P0 impact.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No separate sibling race beyond the already registered failed-launch stranding path was found in the demand handler.

searchLedger.invariant: Bridge probes used for lease/reap decisions should distinguish responsive daemons and healthy/loading model servers without creating a separate respawn race.

searchActions.result: daemon lease bridge uses deepProbe JSON-RPC reply; model-server health probe treats ready/loading as alive and error/wedged/non-response as dead within a timeout.
prepare path probes an existing socket before lock acquisition and releases the demand lock on listen failure; no new bridge-only race found.

searchLedger.rationale: No new bridge probe race found; the actionable new issue is idle eviction root termination in model-server supervision.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Probe behavior has bounded timeouts and liveness distinctions; the reviewed bridge code did not add a new finding beyond known lifecycle issues.

searchLedger.invariant: Crash-loop give-up cooldown should suppress spawn storms and resume after recovery without masking idle ownership failures.

searchActions.result: Cooldown is read before demand spawn and returns crash-loop-cooldown; ready health clears cooldown in prepare path.
Tests cover demand suppression during cooldown and cooldown arming after wedged reap.

searchLedger.rationale: No separate give-up/cooldown finding was found in this pass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The cooldown branches are present and covered; they do not mitigate the new idle root-termination gap.

title: Idle eviction clears ownership without terminating the model-server root process

claim: Idle eviction can clear model-server ownership without killing the model-server root process.
counterevidenceSought: Checked whether reapProcessTree signals the root, whether clearTimers kills the child, whether idle tests assert root termination, and whether explicit respawn reaping has root-aware behavior.
alternativeExplanation: The helper name suggests process-tree reaping, but the implementation excludes childPid and idle eviction never sends a separate root signal.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if another production mechanism proves the root model-server process exits before shared ownership is cleared.

claim: Lazy model-server respawn locks expire while the live listener still owns them.
counterevidenceSought: Checked stale predicate, long-lived listener lock contract, and tests preserving aged-live lock reclaim.
alternativeExplanation: Age-based reclaim may be intended as a hung-owner backstop, but it conflicts with a long-lived listener lease.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if another guard prevents a second launcher from reaching acquireDemandRespawnLock after the TTL while the first listener remains live.

claim: Demand-triggered model-server spawn failures can strand the lazy listener after listener teardown.
counterevidenceSought: Checked failed-launch re-arm, finally-protected lock release, and whether launch failure is impossible in supported runtimes.
alternativeExplanation: No-pid spawn may be rare with process.execPath, but the code explicitly handles it by returning false after the listener is removed.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if supported runtimes guarantee no-pid/synchronous spawn failure cannot happen, or another production path immediately re-arms on launched:false.

focus: cache + reindex + router deep: eviction/store ordering, partial chunk failure, profile-key drift

graphEvents.label: P1 router adapter cache omits embedding dimension

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Iteration 013 rechecks cache migration/eviction, reindex batch/shard writes, and router dimension/profile isolation across committed embedding-stack code.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Prompt-selected high-risk surfaces: cache eviction/migration/profile key behavior, reindex batch loop and shard write ordering, and execution-router count+byte chunking/profile dimension isolation.

findingDetails.title: Factory-backed adapter cache ignores dimensions, so same provider/model profile swaps can reuse the wrong embedding dimension
findingDetails.claim: getEmbedderAdapter resolves dimensionsOverride but caches factory-backed adapters under provider:model only. If the same provider/model is used with a different active dimension in the same process, the router returns the old adapter/provider, while callers and cache profile keys believe they are operating under the new dimension.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked router tests for provider/model rotation, dimension resolution, batching, and ready exposure. They assert cache keys such as openai:model-a and voyage:model-b, but do not cover two calls with the same provider/model and different dimensions. Cache lookup/storage does include profile_key and dimensions, which protects stored rows but does not force the router to construct a dimension-correct provider.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Production might never switch the same provider/model between dimensions without clearing process state. The public router accepts dimensionsOverride, active profiles include dimension, and query callers derive profile keys from active dimension, so that assumption is not enforced at the boundary.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if all production callers prove same provider/model cannot be requested with different dimensions in one process, or if profile activation always calls clearEmbedderExecutionRouterState before any same-model dimension change.

searchLedger.invariant: Cache writes should enforce byte budgets after store without corrupting scoped lookup state or evicting unrelated fresh profile data first.

searchActions.result: storeEmbedding writes one scoped row, then runs global/profile/query/entry eviction in LRU order.
Tests cover global, per-profile, query budget enforcement and shrink_memory on deletion.

searchLedger.rationale: No new eviction-vs-store bug found; the post-store enforcement can delete old rows and is covered for the intended budget classes.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct read and targeted tests show scoped rows remain distinct and LRU budget trimming executes after every store.

searchLedger.invariant: Legacy and shard cache migration should preserve or backfill profile_key/input_kind so scoped lookups do not collide across profiles.

searchActions.result: Fresh migration recreates legacy cache with profile_key/input_kind and backfills profile metadata when present.
Shard migration preserves existing profile_key/input_kind when columns exist and defaults missing legacy columns to document profile empty.
Tests cover main legacy backfill and active-shard scoped cache writes/lookups.

searchLedger.rationale: No new cache migration finding; existing migration/default behavior matches legacy-compat expectations and scoped shard writes are covered.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The risky profile/input-kind columns are either preserved/backfilled or intentionally defaulted for legacy rows, with direct tests for main backfill and shard scoped writes.

searchLedger.invariant: A failed batch should not mark the job completed or flip the active embedder unless all rows are embedded and written.

searchActions.result: The loop checks batch cardinality before writes, increments processed only after main and shard writes, and runs active-embedder/status completion in a later transaction.

searchLedger.rationale: No new partial-chunk completion bug found; failures before completion leave the job failed rather than completed or active-swapped.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Completion metadata and active embedder flip happen only after the batch loop exits normally.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The direct adapter cache may conflate same provider/model profiles that differ only by dimension.

searchActions.result: getEmbedderAdapter resolves dimensions but caches and rotates adapters under provider:model only.
Factory-backed adapter/provider captures the resolved dimensions at construction.
Query embedding derives profileKey from active dimension, obtains an adapter with embeddingDim, then stores returned embeddings under that profileKey.
Tests cover dimension resolution and provider/model rotation but no same provider/model with changed dimensions.

searchLedger.rationale: Dimension is part of the active embedding profile and cache identity, but the router cache omits it, so same-model dimension changes can reuse stale providers.

searchLedger.invariant: Router batching should split by count and bytes and throw before returning partial/null embeddings.

searchActions.result: Router builds non-empty chunks under max count and byte budget, calls embedBatch once per chunk, and throws on null slots.
Tests cover count splitting, byte splitting, order preservation, and null rejection.

searchLedger.rationale: No partial chunking bug found in the router batch assembly/return path.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct read and tests cover count cap, byte cap, output order, and null failure.

focus: retention + governance transaction-boundary sweep

graphEvents.label: P1 retention sweep stale-candidate delete can override rescued delete_after

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Iteration 014 targets destructive retention deletion, governance audit/history writes, FTS maintenance, vacuum, WAL checkpointing, and cross-process/file-backed writer transaction boundaries.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected the retention sweep implementation and its direct delete, audit, ledger, handler, scheduler, and test consumers because the prompt focuses on destructive delete transaction boundaries and post-delete maintenance ordering.

findingDetails.title: Retention sweep deletes stale candidates without revalidating delete_after inside the transaction
findingDetails.claim: Expired rows are selected before the sweep transaction, then deleted by id inside the transaction without confirming the row is still expired, so a concurrent retention-policy update can be lost as a destructive delete.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether candidate selection occurs inside sweepTx, whether deleteMemory enforces a delete_after predicate, whether handler/session callers serialize retention sweeps behind a visible single-writer guard, and whether tests cover updating an already-selected expired row before deletion.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Same-process JavaScript cannot interleave between selection and transaction without an await, but file-backed/multi-process writers remain possible and the existing test suite already models another file-backed writer for retention behavior.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if all memory metadata updates and retention sweeps are proven to run under a process-wide exclusive writer lock, or if selection and deletion are moved into the same immediate write transaction with an expiry predicate.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A row selected as expired can have delete_after extended or cleared before the sweep transaction deletes it, but the sweep still deletes the stale id.

searchActions.result: Expired rows are selected before sweepTx, and sweepTx deletes each candidate id without rereading delete_after.
delete_memory_from_database deletes memory_index by id; no delete_after or retention predicate is enforced in the lower delete path.
Manual and scheduled retention sweeps invoke the same function on the live database without a visible transaction-local candidate refresh.
Concurrent coverage inserts a future row through another connection, but does not update an already-selected expired candidate before deletion.

searchLedger.rationale: The destructive retention predicate is not transaction-local, so governance updates can be overwritten by a stale retention delete.

searchLedger.invariant: FTS optimize, incremental vacuum, and WAL checkpoint should run only after the delete transaction commits.

searchActions.result: runPostDeleteMaintenance is called after sweepTx returns and only when deletedIds is non-empty.
Tests assert optimize, auto_vacuum, and wal_checkpoint run with db.inTransaction false and are skipped when no rows are deleted.

searchLedger.rationale: No maintenance-ordering bug found; the calls run after transaction completion.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct code and tests prove post-delete maintenance occurs outside the sweep transaction.

searchLedger.invariant: Primary memory, vector, cache, FTS-triggered, and ancillary deletes should not partially commit before the sweep transaction can roll back.

searchActions.result: The sweep wraps deletes in sweepTx; delete_memory_from_database performs vector/ancillary/cache deletes before deleting memory_index by id, and FTS cleanup is trigger-driven from the primary delete.
Only BM25 document removal happens after the delete transaction returns, and it is explicitly best-effort after a successful primary delete.

searchLedger.rationale: No separate partial-primary-row corruption finding beyond the stale-candidate predicate bug.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Primary SQLite deletes remain inside transaction scope; post-transaction BM25 cleanup is non-authoritative and best-effort.

searchLedger.invariant: Retention deletes should leave at least one durable governance/audit signal and report ledger failure to callers.

searchActions.result: History and governance audit writes occur in the sweep transaction; mutation-ledger append is attempted after deleted ids accumulate.
Handler reports a hint when mutation ledger append fails instead of hiding ledgerRecorded=false.

searchLedger.rationale: No new governance-ledger P1 found; ledger failure is surfaced and governance audit/history rows remain transaction-bound.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The review did not find an unreported delete path from ledger failure.
